<module id="serial2parallel">
    <description>Implement a series-parallel conversion circuit.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low).</input>
        <input id="din_serial">Serial input data.</input>
        <input id="din_valid">Validity signal for input data.</input>
        <output id="dout_parallel" width_description="[7:0]">Parallel output data.</output>
        <output id="dout_valid">Validity signal for the output data.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter name="cnt">Counter to keep track of the number of received serial input data bits.</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <implementation>
        <sequential_logic>
            <if>
                <condition>
                    <equals>
                        <attribute name="cnt"/>
                        <constant value="3'd7"/>
                    </equals>
                </condition>
                <then>
                    <assign>
                        <attribute name="dout_parallel"/>
                        <attribute name="din_serial"/>
                    </assign>
                    <assign>
                        <attribute name="dout_valid"/>
                        <constant value="1'b1"/>
                    </assign>
                </then>
                <else>
                    <assign>
                        <attribute name="dout_valid"/>
                        <constant value="1'b0"/>
                    </assign>
                </else>
            </if>
        </sequential_logic>
    </implementation>
</module>

<module id="div_8bit">
    <description>Implement a simplified radix-2 divider on 8-bit signed or unsigned integers. and the inputs are two 8-bit operands. The module accepts a dividend and a divisor as inputs and provides a 16-bit result containing both the quotient and the remainder. The design supports both signed and unsigned division operations.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock.</input>
        <input id="rst">Reset.</input>
        <input id="sign">1-bit indicates if the operation is signed (1) or unsigned (0).</input>
        <input id="dividend" width_description="[7:0]">8-bit input signal representing the dividend for division.</input>
        <input id="divisor" width_description="[7:0]">8-bit input signal representing the divisor for division.</input>
        <input id="opn_valid">1-bit indicates that a valid operation request is present.</input>
        <input id="res_ready">1-bit indicates that the 16-bit output result has already been acknowledged.</input>
        <output id="res_valid">1-bit output signal indicating the result is valid and ready.</output>
        <output id="result" width_description="[15:0]">16-bit the output containing the remainder in the upper 8 bits and the quotient in the lower 8 bits.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <param id="STATE_0">0</param>
        <param id="STATE_1">1</param>
        <param id="STATE_2">2</param>
        <param id="STATE_3">3</param>
    </parameter_description>
    <implementation>
        <para>Implement the divider operation using a sequential algorithm. Reset the internal states when the rst signal is high. If opn_valid is high, perform the division operation. If res_valid is high, output the result.</para>
        <para>Initialize the internal registers and variables. The internal registers include the shift register (SR), the negative divisor (NEG_DIVISOR), the remainder (REM), the quotient (QUO), and the counter (COUNT).</para>
        <para>SR is initialized to the absolute value of the dividend shifted left by one bit.</para>
        <para>NEG_DIVISOR is initialized to the absolute value of the divisor.</para>
        <para>QUO and REM are initialized to zero.</para>
        <para>COUNT is initialized to one.</para>
        <para>On every clock cycle, if the rst signal is high, reset all the internal states.</para>
        <para>Otherwise, if opn_valid is high, perform the division operation.</para>
        <para>Otherwise, if res_valid is high, shift the quotient and remainder into SR.</para>
        <para>Otherwise, perform the subtraction of the divisor from the remainder.</para>
        <para>if the subtraction result is non-negative, set the carry_out to 1 and the subtraction result to the remainder.</para>
        <para>if the subtraction result is negative, set the carry_out to 0 and the remainder to the subtraction result.</para>
        <para>Shift the SR to the left by one bit and insert the carry_out at the least significant bit.</para>
        <para>Increment the counter by one.</para>
        <para>if the counter is equal to 8, set res_valid to 1 and the result to the contents of SR.</para>
        <para>Otherwise, if the counter is not equal to 8, set res_valid to 0.</para>
    </implementation>
</module>

samples.gvd:
module width_8to16(
    input clk,
    input rst_n,
    input valid_in,
    input [7:0] data_in,
    output reg valid_out,
    output reg [15:0] data_out
);
    reg [7:0] data_lock;
    reg flag;
    always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
        if (!rst_n) begin
            data_out <= 16'd0;
        end else begin
            if (valid_in) begin
                if (!flag) begin
                    data_lock <= data_in;
                end else begin
                    data_out <= {data_lock, data_in};
                    flag <= 1'b0;
                end
            end
        end
    end
    always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
        if (!rst_n) begin
            flag <= 1'b0;
        end else begin
            if (valid_in) begin
                if (!flag) begin
                    flag <= 1'b1;
                end else begin
                    flag <= 1'b0;
                end
            end
        end
    end
    always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
        if (!rst_n) begin
            valid_out <= 1'b0;
        end else begin
            if (valid_in && flag) begin
                valid_out <= 1'b1;
            end else begin
                valid_out <= 1'b0;
            end
        end
    end
endmodule
samples.xml_description:
<module id="width_8to16">
    <description>Converts 8-bit data input to 16-bit data output.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal.</input>
        <input id="valid_in">Input signal indicating the validity of the input data.</input>
        <input id="data_in" width_description="[7:0]">8-bit input data.</input>
        <output id="valid_out">Output signal indicating the validity of the output data.</output>
        <output id="data_out" width_description="[15:0]">16-bit output data.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <enum id="flag">Flag signal for controlling data output.</enum>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="data_lock" width_description="[7:0]">Register for temporarily storing the first valid input data.</reg>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="flag">Flag signal for controlling data output.</reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        <when test="&rst_n">
            <block>
                <case test="&valid_in">
                    <statement test="!flag">data_out <= {data_lock, data_in};</statement>
                    <statement test="flag">valid_out <= 1'b1;</statement>
                </case>
                <case test="&valid_in">
                    <statement test="!flag">flag <= 1'b1;</statement>
                    <statement test="flag">flag <= 1'b0;</statement>
                </case>
                <case test="!&valid_in">
                    <statement test="flag">valid_out <= 1'b1;</statement>
                </case>
            </block>
        </when>
        <else>
            <block>
                <case test="!flag">
                    <statement test="!valid_in">data_out <= 16'd0;</statement>
                </case>
                <case test="flag">
                    <statement test="!valid_in">valid_out <= 1'b0;</statement>
                </case>
            </block>
        </else>
    </implementation>
</module>

module pe(
    input clk,
    input rst,
    input [31:0] a,
    input [31:0] b,
    output reg [31:0] c
);

      c <= 0;

samples.xml_description:
Implement a traffic light with red, yellow, green indicators and a pedestrian button. In normal traffic light operation, the east-west (EW) light should have a 60-clock cycle period, with 5 clock cycles of yellow and 10 clock cycles of red. The north-south (NS) light should have a 60-clock cycle period, with 5 clock cycles of yellow and 10 clock cycles of red. The cycle starts over after the north-south light turns green again. If the pedestrian button is pressed, the east-west light is shortened to 10 clocks if the remaining green time is longer than 10 clocks. If the remaining green time is less than 10 clocks, the east-west light is unchanged. The east-west and north-south lights are paired. The east-west light turns green or yellow at the same time the north-south light turns red. And vice versa. Reset signal (active low). Clock signal. Pedestrian button input. Red output signal. Yellow output signal. Green output signal. Idle state. State 1 red. State 2 yellow. State 3 green. next_state state == idle idle 0 0 0 state == s1_red s1_red 1 0 0 state == s2_yellow s2_yellow 0 1 0 state == s3_green s3_green 0 0 1 next_cnt !rst_n 10 pass_request && green 10 !green && p_green 60 !yellow && p_yellow 5 !red && p_red 10 The first always block is responsible for the state transition logic. It uses a case statement to handle different states. Here's a summary of each state:
idle: Initial state where all signals are set to 0. Transition to s1_red state occurs immediately.
s1_red: Sets the red signal to 1 and waits for a count of 3 before transitioning to s3_green state. Otherwise, it remains in s1_red state.
s2_yellow: Sets the yellow signal to 1 and waits for a count of 3 before transitioning to s1_red state. Otherwise, it remains in s2_yellow state.
s3_green: Sets the green signal to 1 and waits for a count of 3 before transitioning to s2_yellow state. Otherwise, it remains in s3_green state.

The second always block handles the counting logic of the internal counter (cnt). The counter is decremented by 1 on every positive edge of the clock or negative edge of the reset signal. The counter values are adjusted based on various conditions:
If (!rst_n), the counter is set to 10.
If the pass_request signal is active and the green signal is active, the counter is set to 10.
If the green signal is inactive and the previous green signal (p_green) was active, the counter is set to 60.
If the yellow signal is inactive and the previous yellow signal (p_yellow) was active, the counter is set to 5.
If the red signal is inactive and the previous red signal (p_red) was active, the counter is set to 10.
Otherwise, the counter is decremented normally.

The assign statement assigns the value of the internal counter (cnt) to the output clock.

The final always block handles the output signals. It assigns the previous values (p_red, p_yellow, p_green) to the output signals (red, yellow, green) on the positive edge of the clock or negative edge of the reset signal.
 endmodule

A register that stores the accumulated sum of partial products. A register that stores the final product. A wire that stores the partial products. A wire that stores the result of bitwise AND operation. A wire that stores the result of bitwise OR operation. A wire that stores the result of bitwise XOR operation. On the positive edge of the clock signal, the module samples the input enable signal and stores it in a register, mul_en_out_reg. On the positive edge of the clock signal, the module samples the input values and stores them in the corresponding registers, mul_a_reg and mul_b_reg. The module generates partial products by performing bitwise operations between the multiplicand and the individual bits of the multiplier. It uses conditional assignments to select the appropriate bits from the multiplicand based on the multiplier bits. The partial products are stored in a set of wires, temp. The module performs addition operations on the partial products to calculate partial sums. It uses a set of registers, sum, to store the intermediate sum values. Each register corresponds to a group of partial products and is updated on the positive edge of the clock signal. The module sums up all the partial sums to obtain the final product. It uses a register, mul_out_reg, to store the accumulated sum. On the positive edge of the clock signal, the module updates the register with the sum of all partial sums. samples.xml_description:
Implement a module for parallel-to-serial conversion, where every four input bits are converted to a serial one bit output (from MSB to LSB). The output signal valid_out is set to 1 to indicate the availability of valid serial output. When valid_out = 1, the most significant bit of d is output, and the remaining three bits are output sequentially in the following 3 cycles. Clock signal used for synchronous operations. Reset signal. Defined as 0 for reset and 1 for reset signal inactive. 4-bit parallel data input. Valid signal indicating the availability of serial output. Serial output representing the converted data. Width of parallel data input. Count of cycles for serial output. Counter register for serial output. Valid signal register for serial output. Data register for parallel data input.

Parallel-to-serial conversion is performed by shifting the data register (data) and outputting the most significant bit (MSB) to the serial output (dout) in a sequential manner. The valid signal (valid) is set to 1 when the last bit of data is output, and the counter (cnt) is reset to 0. Otherwise, the counter (cnt) is incremented by 1, the valid signal (valid) is set to 0, and the data register (data) is shifted left by 1 bit.

When the reset signal (rst_n) is active (high), the counter (cnt) is incremented by 1 on each clock cycle.

<module id="edge_detect">
    <description>Implement a module for edge detection.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low).</input>
        <input id="a">Input signal.</input>
        <output id="rise">Output signal indicating a rising edge.</output>
        <output id="down">Output signal indicating a falling edge.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <enum id="state" width_description="[1:0]">
            <enum-value id="ZERO" description="State ZERO">0</enum-value>
            <enum-value id="ONE" description="State ONE">1</enum-value>
        </enum>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" width_description="[1:0]">
            <reg-value id="state">State.</reg-value>
        </reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
    always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
        if (!rst_n) begin
            state <= ZERO;
        end else begin
            case (state)
                ZERO: begin
                    if (a == 1'b1) begin
                        state <= ONE;
                    end
                end
                ONE: begin
                    if (a == 1'b0) begin
                        state <= ZERO;
                    end
                end
            endcase
        end
    end
    </implementation>
    <task>
        <enum-value id="ZERO" description="State ZERO">0</enum-value>
        <enum-value id="ONE" description="State ONE">1</enum-value>

samples.xml_description:
Implement a 4-bit multiplier with pipeline registers. Clock. Active-low reset. Multiplicand. Multiplier. Product. 4 The input signals (mul_a and mul_b) are extended by adding "size" number of zero bits at the most significant bit positions.

Multiplication operation:

The module uses a generate block to perform multiplication for each bit position of the multiplier (mul_b) and generate the partial products.

For each bit position i from 0 to size-1, the partial product is calculated as follows:

If the corresponding bit in the multiplier is 1, the multiplicand is left-shifted by i positions.

If the corresponding bit in the multiplier is 0, the partial product is set to 0 ('d0).

Add of partial products:

The module uses registers to store the intermediate sum values.

On the positive edge of the clock signal (clk) or the falling edge of the reset signal (rst_n), the module performs add operations.

If the reset signal (rst_n) is low, indicating a reset condition, the registers are set to 0.

If the reset signal (rst_n) is high, the registers are updated with the sum of the corresponding partial products.

Final product calculation:

On the positive edge of the clock signal (clk) or the falling edge of the reset signal (rst_n), the module calculates the final product.

If the reset signal (rst_n) is low, indicating a reset condition, the product output (mul_out) is set to 0.

If the reset signal (rst_n) is high, the product output (mul_out) is updated with the sum of registers.

samples.xml_description:
Implement an asynchronous FIFO, FIFO bit width and depth can be configured(parameter DEPTH = 16, parameter WIDTH = 8). The asynchronous FIFO structure is divided into several parts. The first part is dual-port RAM, which is used for data storage. Instantiate dual-port RAM as a submodule, The RAM ports are input wclk, input wenc, input [$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0] waddr, input [WIDTH-1:0] wdata, input rclk, input renc, input [$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0] raddr, output reg [WIDTH-1:0] rdata. The second part is the data write controller. The third part is the data read controller. The fourth part is the read pointer synchronizer. The read pointer is collected using the two-stage trigger of the write clock and output to the data write controller. The fifth part is the write pointer synchronizer, which uses the two-stage trigger of the read clock to collect the write pointer and output it to the data read controller. Write clock. Read clock. Reset signal. Write increment. Read increment. Write data. Write full. Read empty. Read data. Data width of FIFO. Depth of FIFO.

Asynchronous FIFO is composed of four parts. First is dual-port RAM. The FIFO data is stored in the RAM. Instantiate dual-port RAM as a submodule. The RAM ports are input wclk, input wenc, input [$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0] waddr, input [WIDTH-1:0] wdata, input rclk, input renc, input [$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0] raddr, output reg [WIDTH-1:0] rdata.

Second part is data write controller. It contains write pointer, write full, write address and write increment. The write pointer is incremented by write increment. When write pointer is full, write full is 1. The write address is the lower three bits of the write pointer. The write increment is 1 when write enable and no full.

Third part is data read controller. It contains read pointer, read empty, read address and read increment. The read pointer is incremented by read increment. When read pointer is equal to write pointer, read empty is 1. The read address is the lower three bits of the read pointer. The read increment is 1 when read enable and no empty.

Fourth part is read pointer synchronizer. It contains two-stage trigger of read clock. It is used to collect write pointer and output to data write controller.

Fifth part is write pointer synchronizer. It contains two-stage trigger of write clock. It is used to collect read pointer and output to data read controller.

Full and empty signals are compared by Gray code. Use 4-bit Gray code as a read/write pointer for a FIFO with depth 8. The gray code is converted to a four-digit binary number. The binary number is the address to access RAM. When the read and write Pointers are equal, the FIFO is null. When the write pointer has one more cycle RAM than the read pointer, the highest and second-highest bits of the read and write pointer are opposite, the remaining bits are the same, and the FIFO is full.

<module id="pulse_detect">
    <description>Implement a module for pulse detection.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low).</input>
        <input id="data_in">1-bit input signal.</input>
        <output id="data_out">Output signal indicating the presence of pulses.</output>
    </input>
    <parameter_description>
        <enum id="S0">State 0 of the pulse detection FSM.</enum>
        <enum id="S1">State 1 of the pulse detection FSM.</enum>
        <enum id="S2">State 2 of the pulse detection FSM.</enum>
        <enum id="S3">State 3 of the pulse detection FSM.</enum>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="state">FSM state register.</reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        <always>
            <case>
                <case_item value="~rst_n"><reg>state</reg> = <enum>S0</enum>; <output>data_out</output> = 0; break;</case_item>
                <case_item value="<enum>S0"><reg>state</reg> = <enum>S1</enum>; <output>data_out</output> = 0; break;</case_item>
                <case_item value="<enum>S1">if (<input>data_in</input> == 1) <reg>state</reg> = <enum>S2</enum>; else <reg>state</reg> = <enum>S0</enum>; <output>data_out</output> = 0; break;</case_item>
                <case_item value="<enum>S2"><reg>state</reg> = <enum>S3</enum>; <output>data_out</output> = 0; break;</case_item>
                <case_item value="<enum>S3">if (<input>data_in</input> == 0) <reg>state</reg> = <enum>S0</enum>; else <reg>state</reg> = <enum>S0</enum>; <output>data_out</output> = 1; break;</case_item>
                <case_item value="default"><reg>state</reg> = <enum>S0</enum>; <output>data_out</output> = 0; break;</case_item>
            </case>
        </always>
    </implementation>
</module>

<module id="accu">
    <description>Accumulate 4 input data and output the result.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock input for synchronization.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal.</input>
        <input id="data_in" width_description="[7:0]">8-bit input data for addition.</input>
        <input id="valid_in">Input signal indicating readiness for new data.</input>
        <output id="valid_out">Output signal indicating when 4 input data accumulation is reached.</output>
        <output id="data_out" width_description="[9:0]">10-bit output data representing the accumulated sum.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <enum id="state" width_description="[1:0]">
            <enum-value id="idle" value="0">Idle state, waiting for valid_in.</enum-value>
            <enum-value id="accumulate" value="1">Accumulate state, ready to receive data_in.</enum-value>
        </enum>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <reg id="valid_out_reg" type="sequential_logic">A register to store the valid_out value.</reg>
        <reg id="data_out_reg" width_description="[9:0]" type="sequential_logic">A register to store the accumulated sum.</reg>
        <reg id="state_reg" width_description="[1:0]" type="sequential_logic">A register to store the current state.</reg>
        <reg id="count_reg" width_description="[1:0]" type="sequential_logic">A register to store the count of received data_in.</reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        <item>On a rising edge of clk or a falling edge of rst_n:</item>
        <item>Reset valid_out_reg and data_out_reg when rst_n is low.</item>
        <item>Change state when valid_in is high:</item>
        <item>
            <item>Idle:</item>
            <item>Transition to accumulate when valid_in is 1.</item>
        </item>
        <item>
            <item>Accumulate:</item>
            <item>Accumulate data_in and update data_out_reg.</item>
            <item>Count the number of received data_in.</item>
            <item>When 4 data_in is received:</item>
            <item>
                <item>Set valid_out_reg to 1.</item>
                <item>Reset state to idle.</item>
                <item>Reset count_reg.</item>
            </item>
        </item>
    </implementation>
</module>

    input rst_n,
    input write_en,
    input [2:0] write_addr,
    input [5:0] write_data,
    input read_en,
    input [2:0] read_addr,
    output [5:0] read_data
);

reg [5:0] mem [0:7];

always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin

        for (int i = 0; i < 8; i++) begin
            mem[i] <= 6'b000000;
        end

        end

end

<module id="synchronizer">
    <description>Implement a multi-bit MUX-based synchronizer. The data_en input is synchronous to clk_a and the data_in input is constant during the period when data_en=1. So, sample the data_in when data_en=1 and synchronize it to clk_b. The data change frequency of data_in is very low. The change interval between two adjacent data is at least 10 clk_b clock cycles. The clock clk_a is responsible for data_in and data_en. The clock clk_b is responsible for the 2 D flip-flops.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk_a">Clock signal A.</input>
        <input id="clk_b">Clock signal B.</input>
        <input id="arstn">Active-low reset signal A.</input>
        <input id="brstn">Active-low reset signal B.</input>
        <input id="data_in" width_description="[3:0]">Input data.</input>
        <input id="data_en">Input enable.</input>
        <output id="dataout" width_description="[3:0]">Output data.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <enum id="state" width_description="[1:0]">
            <enum_value id="one" value="0">State one.</enum_value>
            <enum_value id="two" value="1">State two.</enum_value>
        </enum>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <reg id="state" type="sequential_logic" width_description="[1:0]">
            <source>
                <case>
                    <when condition="rst_n==0">0</when>
                    <when condition="clk==1">
                        <case>
                            <when condition="state==0">state</when>
                            <when condition="state==1">state+1</when>
                        </case>
                    </when>
                </case>
            </source>
        </reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        <case>
            <when condition="brstn==0">The output data is all 0s.</when>
            <when condition="clk_b==1">
                <case>
                    <when condition="en_clap_two==1">The input data is passed to the output data.</when>
